title: European Commission Blue Book Traineeship
description: A five-month paid traineeship giving young graduates from around the world hands-on experience in EU policymaking and administration, with roughly 1,000 positions split across the Commission's departments, services, and agencies -- most based in Brussels, with some in Luxembourg or national bureaus across EU member states. The programme is open to all EU citizens, with a limited number of places reserved for non-EU nationals (including Eastern Partnership countries) in specific Directorates-General. Applicants must hold a Bachelor's degree or equivalent and be fluent in at least two EU languages, one of which must be English, French, or German. Trainees receive a monthly grant of roughly EUR 1,400-1,540, a flat-rate reimbursement for travel from their home country to Brussels and back, and accident/health insurance; trainees arrange their own accommodation. This entry covers the March 2027 session: registration opened July 22, 2026, with the application deadline September 4, 2026.
visaNote: Non-EU trainees receive visa/residence-permit support from the Commission as part of the programme.
